column4: Addition or modification of technical jargon used by domain experts in the research field of {PAGE_NAME} to the current revision that was not present in the previous revision. This refers to the use of field-specific terms, concepts, or processes that carry a precise meaning recognized by experts in the field, and that would likely be unfamiliar to general audiences without specialized knowledge.
column5: Current revision - Recent research suggests that dietary fiber modulates gut microbiota composition, which influences health.

column4: Inclusion of researcher names in the current revision, but only if they appear directly in the article text outside of reference tags (<ref>). This label should be used when a researcher’s name is explicitly mentioned as part of the main article content, such as in a sentence or paragraph (including phrases like “a study by [researcher name]” or “[researcher name] et al. showed”), but not when it appears only within a citation or reference.
column5: Current revision - Studies by Smith et al. have shown that melatonin affects sleep.

column4: Addition of references to published academic papers in the current revision. These typically include standardized identifiers such as DOI, PMID, PMC, arXiv, or ISSN, which are commonly used to reference scientific publications, or enough information to trace back to a specific academic paper. Such references usually appear within <ref> tags that contain {{cite journal}}. Only include academic papers; exclude URLs, books, and edits to existing references.
column5: Current revision – The future of medicine is promising <ref name="Smith2020">{{Cite journal |last=Smith |first=John |title=The Future of Medicine |journal=Medical Journal |year=2020 |volume=45 |issue=2 |pages=123–130 |doi=10.1016/j.medj.2020.01.001 |pmid=32012345}}</ref>

column4: Addition of a new link to another Wikipedia page in the current revision. Do not include edits or modifications of existing wikilinks.
column5: Current revision - [[Androsthenes of Thasos|Androsthenes]] sailed with [[Nearchus]].

column3: Addition or Modification of Quantitative Information
column4: Addition of statistics or numeric experimental results in the current revision. Qualifying additions include specific metrics, percentages, counts of measured quantities, ratios, confidence intervals, p-values, dosages, or physical measurements (e.g., concentrations, durations, distances) in the main article text. Do not apply Label 10 to dates or years, such as "in 2018" or "the 1700s". Do not apply Label 10 to relative or comparative terms that carry no numeric value, such as "several", "most", "many", "few", or "more than". Do not apply Label 10 to counting words used as ordinals or quantifiers, such as "at least two of the following" or "three criteria". Do not apply Label 10 to numeric metadata inside <ref> tags or {{cite}} templates.
column5: Current revision - affects 7.2% of the population based on newer studies…
